diagrams.title: Race Condition Scenario
diagrams.description: Two threads accessing and modifying a shared variable simultaneously showing how race condition leads to incorrect results
elements: Both write back - value is 11 instead of 12
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how non-atomic operations cause data inconsistency

diagrams.title: Critical Section Model
diagrams.description: Diagram showing the structure of processes with entry section, critical section, and exit section
diagrams.purpose: Understand the structure required for proper synchronization

diagrams.title: Semaphore Operations
diagrams.description: Visual representation of wait() and signal() operations on a semaphore
elements: If value < 0, process added to waiting queue
If value <= 0, wake one waiting process
Queue: FIFO or based on scheduling
diagrams.purpose: Understand how semaphores coordinate process execution

diagrams.title: Deadlock vs Race Condition
diagrams.description: Comparison diagram showing two scenarios: deadlock (circular wait) and race condition (timing-dependent incorrect output)
elements: Deadlock: P1 holds R1, waits for R2; P2 holds R2, waits for R1
Race condition: Timeline showing interleaved operations
Outcome A vs Outcome B depending on timing
diagrams.purpose: Differentiate between these two critical synchronization problems

diagrams.title: Producer-CConsumer Problem
diagrams.description: Bounded buffer with producer and consumer processes showing synchronization requirements
elements: Producer: produces item, checks 'empty' semaphore, adds to buffer, signals 'full'
Consumer: checks 'full' semaphore, removes item, signals 'empty'
diagrams.purpose: Understand classic synchronization problem with semaphores
